Send & Receive
click to open · chrome extensions handle the rest
How It Works
📡
Daily Runner
Runs daily-comms.py to snapshot all your comms — Gmail, iMessage, WhatsApp, and Messenger — into one JSON feed.

It diffs against yesterday's snapshot so you only see what's new. Run it once in the morning and your dashboard lights up with fresh data.

Think of it as your daily pulse check. One script, all channels, zero manual work.
📬
Outbox Server
The outbox-server.py runs on port 3003 and handles outbound messages using a tee-up then go flow.

Stage a message, review it in the dashboard, then hit Go to send across 4 channels: Gmail, iMessage, WhatsApp, or Messenger.

Nothing sends until you say so. Full control, zero surprises.
🎯
Client Journey
Every client gets a journey card with 4 punch dots — each one marks a milestone in the pipeline.

From first contact to signed, the dots fill in as you move them through. Click the card to see details, history, and next steps.

It's your visual tracker for where every relationship stands.
🚀
Client App Launcher
The launcher is this panel right here — your workspace hub with colored screens.

Blue is Social Climate Organizer for ID verification. Green is Payment Portals. Purple is the Label Generator. Red is EDM Trending Tracker.

Each dot up top switches screens. Your last-used screen is remembered.
⚡
Quick Action Buttons
The toolbar along the bottom of the dashboard has quick-fire buttons for common tasks.

Theme switcher, pill style cycler, the Space Bar comms panel, and the launcher button — all one click away.

They stay visible in every view so you always have fast access.
🦅
Left Wing
The left wing is your incoming feed — pills for Gmail, iMessage, WhatsApp, and Messenger.

Each pill expands to show submenu cards you can click-to-open or drag to the center drop zone.

Data refreshes automatically from the scraper feeds in data/feeds/.
🦅
Right Wing
The right wing holds your action-oriented tools — pipeline stages, proposal tracking, and client management.

Pills here represent workflow steps. Expand them to see active items and take action directly from the card view.

It mirrors the left wing layout so everything feels consistent and fast.
